name: Quick Soda Crackers
recipeInstructions:
These crackers cannot truly duplicate the lightness of traditional soda crackers, but they take a fraction of the time to make and are delicious on their own. They pair well with soups or stews.
Preheat the oven to 350°F.
In a food processor or large bowl, combine the flour, salt, and baking powder. Add the shortening and cut it into the flour until the mixture resembles coarse meal. Gradually add water and mix until a dough forms that holds together in a cohesive ball.
Divide the dough into three equal portions for rolling. On a floured surface or pastry cloth, roll out each portion to a rectangle about 1/4 inch thick, with the long edge running horizontally in front of you.
Fold the left third of the dough over the center third. Fold the right third over the center. The dough is now divided into three layers with the seam running vertically.
Rotate the dough so the seam runs horizontally. Roll out again to a rectangle about 1/4 inch thick. Fold and turn the dough as in step 5.
Roll the dough thinner this time, about 1/8 inch thick. If desired, sprinkle the top lightly and evenly with salt and roll it over gently with the rolling pin.
Using a sharp knife, cut into 2-inch squares and place them on an ungreased baking sheet. Prick each square 2 or 3 times with the tines of a fork.
Bake for 25 to 30 minutes, or until lightly browned. Cool on a rack.
Yield: 80-85 crackers
